title: A ReaxFF-based thermomechanical analysis of N-carbophenes: Phase-change, thermal expansion, and high temperature synthesis pathway
abstract:

Author(s): Chad E. Junkermeier, Kat Lavarez, R. Martin Adra, Valeria Aparicio Diaz, Heather Osterstock, Pal Casinto, M. Verano, Ricardo Paupitz, Adri C.T. van Duin
relevance_reason: ReaxFF classical force-field MD applied to thermomechanical properties of N-carbophenes; satisfies (a) explicit atoms, (b) classical MD with ReaxFF, (c) method application to materials system.

title: Elucidating the electronic mechanisms behind the opposite roles of Cu and Cr in adhesion mechanism and vacancy formation at the NiF<sub>2</sub>/Ni interface

title: Best practice and validation for interatomic potentials of molecular dynamics modeling of ice in planetary science
abstract:
Publication date: 5 June 2026
Source: Computational Materials Science, Volume 270
Author(s): Vikentiy Pashuk, Ivan Saika-Voivod, Rocky Taylor, Liam S. Morrissey

title: Unveiling nonlinear descriptors via hierarchical learning for single-atom catalysts
abstract:
Author(s): Liangliang Xu, Ning Xu, Yan Wang, Yiyan Jin, Xiaojuan Hu, Linguo Lu, Xin Tan, Zhongfang Chen, and Zhong-Kang Han
Single-atom catalysts (SACs) hold great promise for CO-selective NO reduction (CO-SCR) due to their maximal metal atom utilization and the tunable adsorption of reactants and intermediates. However, optimizing SACs remains a major challenge because it requires a delicate balance of adsorption streng…
[Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 045802] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
relevance_reason: Machine-learning descriptor work for single-atom catalysts (SACs) with DFT calculations for adsorption/catalysis; systems are atomic, DFT and MLIP-style descriptor learning are in-scope, and application is to materials discovery—satisfies (a)–(c) as MLIP/DFT-driven SAC optimization.

title: Free-energy analysis of bubble nucleation on electrocatalytic surfaces
abstract:
Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys., 2026, 28,10792-10799
Qingguang Xie, Paolo Malgaretti, Othmane Aouane, Simon Thiele, Jens Harting
We present a free-energy model that quantitatively predicts both the activation energy and critical nucleus size of bubbles at given supersaturation, temperature, pressure, and surface wettability.
relevance_reason: Free-energy modeling of bubble nucleation on electrocatalytic surfaces with quantitative prediction of activation barriers; satisfies (a) atomic/molecular system, (b) free-energy method (nucleation theory applied to surfaces), (c) application to interfacial phenomenon; solid free-energy calculation, though theoretical model rather than full MD.

title: Scalable learning of macroscopic stochastic dynamics
abstract:
Author(s): Mengyi Chen, Pengru Huang, Kostya S. Novoselov, and Qianxiao Li
Macroscopic dynamical descriptions are essential for understanding and controlling complex material behavior, yet deriving them from microscopic simulations remains computationally prohibitive for spatially extended stochastic systems. To address this challenge, the authors propose a machine-learning framework that learns large-scale macroscopic dynamics using only small-system simulations. The method uses a partial evolution scheme to generate training data within local patches, a tailored loss to learn the macroscopic dynamics, and a hierarchical upsampling strategy to efficiently construct large-system configurations. Across stochastic PDEs, lattice spin models, and an NbMoTa alloy system, the framework demonstrates high accuracy, robustness, and computational efficiency.
[Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 033805] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
relevance_reason: ML framework learns macroscopic dynamics from atomistic simulations (lattice spin models, NbMoTa alloy); uses small-system MD/MC data to predict large-scale behavior; satisfies (a) explicit atoms/beads, (b) trained on atomistic simulation outputs, (c) applies learned model to material systems—but contribution is primarily methodological upscaling rather than MLIP development or direct atomistic simulation advance.

title: $5d$-mediated indirect exchange and effective spin Hamiltonians in Ce triangular-lattice delafossites
abstract:
Author(s): Leonid V. Pourovskii
Anisotropic intersite exchange interactions in frustrated rare-earth magnets are difficult to assess both theoretically and experimentally. Here, we propose an ab initio force-theorem framework combining the quasiatomic Hubbard-I approach to $4f$ correlations with a static mean-field treatment of th…
[Phys. Rev. B 113, L201109] Published Fri May 08, 2026
relevance_reason: Ab initio DFT study combining Hubbard-I approach with mean-field treatment to extract effective spin Hamiltonians from first principles; meets (a)–(c): explicit atoms, DFT methodology, derives fundamental parameters for a real magnetic material.

title: Pressure-induced structural phase transition and thermoelectric properties of bulk Mg3Bi2
abstract:
Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys., 2026, 28,10946-10952
Han Zhang, Tao Feng, Xiaolong Gan, Shuoxue Jin, Yujun Zhang, Jiaou Wang, Zhiying Guo, Wei Xu, Weishu Liu
Under high pressure, the thermoelectric material Mg3Bi2 exhibits multiple phase transitions and two newly discovered crystal structures.
relevance_reason: DFT-based computational study of crystal structures and thermoelectric properties under pressure in Mg3Bi2; satisfies (a) explicit atomic system, (b) DFT geometry optimization / structure prediction, (c) applies DFT to materials discovery—within scope as atomistic simulation.
